rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 9, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT TOM KING'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MORTON L. SILVERS et al VS. WEISS & COMPANY, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 9, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT WEISS & COMPANY, LLP'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING HE WAS A 3RD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE ENGAGEMENT CONTRACT BETWEEN MOT AND DEFENDANT. AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ACTIONS, AS DISTINGUISHED FROM UNSPOKEN AND UNRECORDED THOUGHTS AND DECISIONS, THAT WOULD INDICATE THAT PLAINTIFF ACTUALLY RELIED ON THE MISREPRESENTATIONS. SEE SMALL V. FRITZ COMPANIES, INC. (2003) 30 CAL.4TH 167, 184. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW DIMITRIOU VS. CIT FINANCIAL SERVICES INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 9, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (ROSENTHAL ACT) AND 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION (CLRA). THE LEASE SPECIFIES THAT THE COPIER WAS TO BE USED FOR BUSINESS PURPOSES ONLY, AND THE COPIER WAS NOT USED PRIMARILY FOR PERSONAL, FAMILY OR HOUSEHOLD PURPOSES. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND: AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (B&P CODE SECTION 17200) FOR PLAINTIFF TO CLEARLY STATE WHY AND HOW DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS WERE UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR OR FRAUDULENT. AS TO 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION (FRAUD) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WHO, WHEN, WHAT AND HOW THE MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE, RELIANCE AND DAMAGES. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 2ND, AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW DIMITRIOU VS. CIT FINANCIAL SERVICES INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 9, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CIT TECHNOLOGY FINANCING SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE DAMAGES IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 425.10(a). MOTION DENIED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK DE BIBO VS. VICTOR PENA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 9, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS VICTOR PENA AND MONICA MARTINEZ DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IF REFERENCED AS AN EXHIBIT, THE WRITTEN AGREEMENTS MUST BE ATTACHED TO THE PLEADINGS. FRAUD MUST BE PLED WITH PARTICULARITY, FACTUALLY AND SPECIFICALLY. SEE LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 638. =(302/CWW)
